mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a hit rate above 55%, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply chain bottlenecks and manufacturing weakness, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during inflationary periods.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 4.0%, or 5-day forward returns during industrial contractions show no significant deviation from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, elevated financing costs compress equity valuations and trigger immediate portfolio de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC turns negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to yield a positive hit rate above 58% over 20-day horizons, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during housing starts expansions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals immediate manufacturing weakness and inventory drawdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45%.
